expectations.text: Says to translate comments inside code blocks
expectations.evidence: Translate all comments (lines starting with #) into Portuguese. Comments are the explanatory text for the reader and should be fully localized.

expectations.text: Says to keep function names and R keywords (function, if, library, etc.) in English
expectations.evidence: Covers function names (mean(), filter(), etc.) but does not mention R language keywords like if, else, for, function

expectations.text: Says variable names may be translated only where it aids understanding
expectations.evidence: translate only if pedagogically necessary and done consistently

expectations.text: Notes the code must still run after translation
expectations.evidence: Translating them would break the code.

expectations.text: Emphasizes translating meaning/intent rather than literally
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Register the language in config/_default/languages.yaml (with languageName and weight)
expectations.evidence: Add an `it` block to `config/_default/languages.yaml`... `languageName: "Italiano"` ... `weight: 5`

expectations.text: Copy i18n/en.yaml to i18n/it.yaml and translate the values, keeping the keys in English
expectations.evidence: `cp i18n/en.yaml i18n/it.yaml` ... Keep the keys in English and the YAML indentation intact.

expectations.text: Add a 'Read in' badge label in data/read_in_lang.yaml
expectations.evidence: In `data/read_in_lang.yaml`, add: `it: "Leggi in italiano"`

expectations.text: Add localized month names (data/months)
expectations.evidence: `cp data/months/en.yaml data/months/it.yaml` Translate each month name so dates render in prose as "12 Giugno 2026".

expectations.text: Mentions the disableLanguages production gate (not live until its code is removed)
expectations.evidence: not live in production until you remove `it` from `disableLanguages` in `config/production/hugo.yaml`

expectations.text: Open a PR tagging @rladies/translation
expectations.evidence: Commit and open a PR tagging `@rladies/translation`.

expectations.text: Mentions the i18n-check coverage action
expectations.evidence: The `i18n-check` workflow checks two things... It posts an "i18n Coverage" comment on the PR listing gaps.

expectations.text: Register the language in config/_default/languages.yaml (with languageName and weight)
expectations.evidence: References `config.toml` or `config/_default/languages.toml` (TOML, not YAML); never names `languages.yaml`

expectations.text: Copy i18n/en.yaml to i18n/it.yaml and translate the values, keeping the keys in English
expectations.evidence: Mentions creating `i18n/it.yaml` with `en.yaml` as reference, but does not say to copy the file or state that keys must stay in English

expectations.text: Add a 'Read in' badge label in data/read_in_lang.yaml
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Add localized month names (data/months)
expectations.evidence: Mentions date/locale formatting generically but never references `data/months`

expectations.text: Mentions the disableLanguages production gate (not live until its code is removed)
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Open a PR tagging @rladies/translation
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Mentions the i18n-check coverage action
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Says to find auto-translated pages via local hugo server / the orange banner
expectations.evidence: hugo server ... confirm the FAQ page shows the orange banner

expectations.text: Edits the content/<section>/<slug>/index.es.md file
expectations.evidence: content/about-us/faq/index.es.md

expectations.text: Translates reader-facing frontmatter (title, description, summary)
expectations.evidence: Translate the reader-facing frontmatter fields — `title`, `description`, and `summary`.

expectations.text: Says NOT to translate date, directory_id, or image filenames
expectations.evidence: Leave technical fields (`date`, `directory_id`, image filenames, etc.) untouched.

expectations.text: Says to remove the `translated: auto` flag when done
expectations.evidence: Remove `translated: auto` from the frontmatter (or change it to `translated: true`). This is what makes the orange banner disappear.

expectations.text: Opens a PR tagging @rladies/translation
expectations.evidence: Open a PR and tag **@rladies/translation** for review.

expectations.text: Mentions inclusive / gender-neutral language conventions
expectations.evidence: Use gender-neutral forms where they fit, consistent with the conventions already used in other Spanish pages on the site.

expectations.text: Mentions translating code comments but keeping function names/keywords in English
expectations.evidence: keep all function names, arguments, and R keywords in English (the code must still run); translate only the comments inside the code blocks.

expectations.text: Says to find auto-translated pages via local hugo server / the orange banner
expectations.evidence: Mentions the orange banner exists and hugo server for preview, but does not explain using the banner or hugo server as a mechanism to find/discover auto-translated pages.

expectations.text: Edits the content/<section>/<slug>/index.es.md file
expectations.evidence: Gives uncertain alternatives: 'content/faq/index.es.md, or content/es/faq/_index.md (or index.md)' — the canonical content/<section>/<slug>/index.es.md pattern is not clearly stated.

expectations.text: Translates reader-facing frontmatter (title, description, summary)
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'translated front-matter fields (like title or description)' but omits summary; partial mention does not satisfy the strict requirement.

expectations.text: Says NOT to translate date, directory_id, or image filenames
expectations.evidence: absent — only says 'Keep the YAML front matter intact' with no specific fields named.

expectations.text: Says to remove the `translated: auto` flag when done
expectations.evidence: Says to remove 'auto_translated: true' — wrong field name; the exact flag `translated: auto` is never mentioned.

expectations.text: Opens a PR tagging @rladies/translation
expectations.evidence: Says 'Request a review from a Spanish-speaking R-Ladies maintainer if you know one' — @rladies/translation team is not mentioned.

expectations.text: Mentions inclusive / gender-neutral language conventions
expectations.evidence: absent

expectations.text: Mentions translating code comments but keeping function names/keywords in English
expectations.evidence: absent

Skill lifts pass rate by +70% on average (with=95%, without=25%); biggest single-eval gap is 'abstract-coordinate' (+100%).
24 assertion(s) pass in BOTH configs - to the extent these match generic packaging vocabulary the benchmark understates the skill's rOpenSci-specific value.

Skill adds 3473 tokens vs baseline on average and runs 6s slower; baseline time/tokens vary run-to-run because without the skill the model explores/searches, while with it the model answers from grounded references.
